_source: Synthetic representative profile for Chez 2002 J Child Neurol (PMID 12585724) L-carnosine RCT. Trial inclusion was broad ASD (ages 3-12), NOT biomarker-stratified. 13/31 had abnormal EEG and 13/31 were on valproic acid — a GABA-axis-loaded subset. L-carnosine's mechanism is GABA modulation via homocarnosine, frontal/temporal cortex protection, anticonvulsant. Maps to PHE-0007 (GABA/Cl⁻ imbalance). EXCLUDED from cohort MAE because (1) paper publishes only continuous outcomes (no dichotomized responder rate) AND (2) PHE-0007 has no biomarker drivers in v0.3 engine. The representative profile encodes the GABA-imbalance phenotype proxy via abnormal EEG + concomitant valproic acid for engine STRUCTURAL-TEST purposes only — and remains relevant once engine v0.4 wires PHE-0007 biomarker drivers (would also unblock rrc_002 Lemonnier).

_engine_expectation.rationale: Subclinical epileptiform EEG + valproic acid concomitant = GABA-axis-loaded subset that L-carnosine's homocarnosine/GABA mechanism would address. Engine v0.3 has no PHE-0007 biomarker drivers (same architectural gap as Lemonnier rrc_002), so engine returns near-baseline 0.20 on this profile rather than the elevated PHE-0007 the trial's mechanism implies. Once engine v0.4 adds PHE-0007 biomarker drivers (epileptiform-EEG, CSF Cl⁻ proxies, GABA/glutamate ratios from MR spectroscopy), this profile should drive PHE-0007 dominance and serve as a paired test with Lemonnier.
